instagram.keyChanges: The Instagram algorithm prioritizes content from accounts users interact with most, with a focus on Reels and live streaming. Additionally, the algorithm rewards creators who post consistently and engage with their audience. Long-form content and IGTV have been de-emphasized in favor of bite-sized Reels and Stories.
editingTips: Use the Rule of Thirds when composing your Reels for more visually appealing shots.
Experiment with Instagram's advanced editing features like 'Magic Edit' to enhance your photos and Reels.
Incorporate text overlays and captions to add context and engagement to your content.
Take advantage of Instagram's AR effects to create interactive and immersive experiences.
postingTips: Post at least 3-5 times per week to maintain a consistent presence and keep your audience engaged.
Experiment with different posting schedules to find the optimal time for your audience (e.g., lunch breaks, peak hours).
Use Instagram Insights to track your performance and adjust your posting schedule accordingly.
Take breaks from posting to avoid flooding your audience's feed and maintain a healthy engagement ratio.
titleTips: Use attention-grabbing, descriptive titles that accurately reflect the content of your post.
Keep titles concise and under 10 words for maximum visibility.
Use emojis and special characters to add visual interest and personality to your titles.
A/B test different title variations to determine which performs best with your audience.
descriptionTips: Write engaging, informative descriptions that encourage users to swipe up or click on your link.
Use a clear call-to-action (CTA) in your description to prompt users to take action.
Keep descriptions concise and scannable, using bullet points or short paragraphs to break up the text.
Optimize your descriptions for search by including relevant keywords and hashtags.
summaries: Boost Engagement with Reels and Live Streaming
Consistency is Key to Instagram Success
Use Advanced Editing Features to Stand Out
Post Strategically to Reach Your Target Audience

youtube-long.keyChanges: YouTube Long algorithm now prioritizes content with high watch time, engagement, and viewer retention. The algorithm also considers video length, with longer content rewarding creators who produce high-quality, in-depth content. Additionally, YouTube Long now takes into account user behavior, such as pause and scrub behavior, to determine video relevance and value.
editingTips: Use a clear, concise intro to grab viewers' attention.
Break up long content into smaller sections with headings and subheadings.
Use high-quality visuals, such as graphics and animations, to enhance the viewing experience.
Incorporate interactive elements, such as polls and quizzes, to increase engagement.
Utilize closed captions and subtitles to improve video accessibility and watch time.
postingTips: Post content consistently to maintain a strong audience presence.
Experiment with different posting frequencies, such as daily or weekly, to find what works best for your audience.
Consider posting during peak hours, such as 12pm-4pm EST, to reach a wider audience.
Use YouTube's built-in analytics to track your viewership and adjust your posting schedule accordingly.
titleTips: Use attention-grabbing keywords in your title, but avoid clickbait.
Make sure your title accurately represents the content of your video.
Keep your title concise, ideally under 60 characters.
Use a clear, legible font to ensure your title is easily readable.
descriptionTips: Use keywords relevant to your content, but avoid keyword stuffing.
Provide a clear summary of the video's content and topic.
Use a conversational tone to engage viewers and encourage watch time.
Include relevant links or tags to help viewers find related content.
summaries: Boost watch time with high-quality, engaging content!
Maximize viewer retention with interactive video elements.
Unlock YouTube Long growth with strategic posting schedules.
Elevate your video's relevance with accurate, concise titles.

facebook-reels.keyChanges: Facebook Reels algorithm now prioritizes content that is visually engaging, authentic, and provides valuable information to users. The algorithm also considers the time spent watching a Reel and rewards content that keeps viewers engaged. Additionally, Facebook Reels now uses AI-driven moderation to detect and remove low-quality content, such as spam or hate speech.
editingTips: Keep it concise: Edit your Reel to 60 seconds or less for maximum engagement
Make it visually appealing: Use high-quality visuals, transitions, and music to keep viewers engaged
Use captions and subtitles: Make your content more accessible and increase watch time
Add a clear call-to-action: Encourage viewers to engage with your content or visit your website
postingTips: Post consistently: Aim for 3-5 Reels per week to maintain audience engagement and grow your following
Experiment with different posting times: Analyze your analytics to determine the best times to post for your audience
Use Facebook's built-in scheduling feature: Plan and schedule your Reels in advance to save time and increase productivity
titleTips: Keep it short and sweet: Use a title that is 6 words or less and accurately reflects the content of your Reel
Use attention-grabbing language: Craft a title that is attention-grabbing and encourages viewers to click and watch
Add relevant keywords: Incorporate relevant keywords into your title to improve discoverability and reach
descriptionTips: Keep it concise: Keep your description brief and to the point, focusing on the key takeaway or call-to-action
Add relevant links: Include links to your website, other social media profiles, or relevant resources to increase engagement and drive traffic
Use a clear and compelling call-to-action: Encourage viewers to engage with your content or visit your website
summaries: Get more views with visually engaging Reels
Boost engagement with authentic and valuable content
Increase watch time with concise and informative Reels
Grow your following with consistent and well-timed Reels
